shellcheck_coverage.inline_directives: present in covered scripts (# shellcheck shell=bash)

frontmatter_validation.pattern_limitation: skills/SKILL.md is literal path (won't catch additional skill files)
frontmatter_validation.convention_enforcement: none (tools: field, required fields not validated)

critical_gaps.impact: 4 of 9 shell scripts (44%) not validated by bash -n or ShellCheck
critical_gaps.recommendation: Extend CI patterns to include root-level .sh files and tests/ directory

critical_gaps.impact: Cannot catch malformed YAML, missing required fields (tools:), or structural errors
critical_gaps.recommendation: Add YAML parser (yq/python) to validate structure and required fields per copilot-instructions.md

critical_gaps.impact: CI verifies install script runs but not that files are correctly placed, permissions set, or PATH configured
critical_gaps.recommendation: Add post-install assertions: check ~/.copilot/agents/, ~/bin/, file permissions, PATH

critical_gaps.gap: No edge case coverage for install
critical_gaps.impact: Reinstall behavior, missing dependencies, and failure modes untested
critical_gaps.recommendation: Add tests for: second install run, missing tmux/python3/jq, permission failures

critical_gaps.impact: Does not verify help text completeness or accuracy
critical_gaps.recommendation: Add content assertions: check for --count, --repo, --metaswarm options in output

recommendations.implementation: Update CI to include: bin/*.sh install.sh quickstart.sh record-demo.sh tests/*.sh
Use yq or python yaml parser to validate required fields (name, tools, etc.)
Run installer twice, test with missing dependencies (via docker or temporary PATH manipulation)
Check that --help output contains expected option descriptions
Specify shellcheck version in CI for reproducible builds

summary: CI validation covers the core happy path (shell syntax, ShellCheck, basic frontmatter, install execution, help smoke test) but has significant coverage gaps: 44% of shell scripts are not validated, frontmatter validation is superficial (first-line check only), install tests lack assertions about actual file placement and permissions, and edge cases (reinstall, missing deps) are untested. Recommendations prioritize extending shell coverage and adding structured YAML validation.
